Stop Users Skipping Login With One Express Middleware
So far we've gone through a bunch of stuff — member registration, login, and checking whether someone's actually logged in. But here's a question: what if you don't want users jumping straight to the
page just by typing the URL into the address bar? That's exactly where middleware comes in.

Normally you don't want users reaching a page just by punching a URL into the browser. You can block that with plain if-checks, sure, but some pages get complicated enough that scattering checks around isn't fun. That's when middleware (the "in-between component", kind of) earns its keep.

We've already covered member registration, login, and how to tell whether someone is logged in. But what if you don't want users jumping straight to the `user` page by typing the URL into the address bar? That's where middleware comes in. In this post I'll show how to gate a whole route behind login with a tiny Express middleware.
